question: tell me about 3 6 9
content:
The 3-6-9 system is a widely used and simple method for shooting spares in bowling. Its core principle is to maintain a consistent target, specifically Arrow 4, and make all adjustments by moving your feet on the approach. The ball should be thrown straight, without any hook, to ensure accuracy. The foundation of this system is the "Zero Position," which is the spot on the approach from where, by throwing the ball straight at Arrow 4, the ball hits the center of the head pin (Pin 1). This Zero Position becomes your unchanging base reference for all spare adjustments.

Common mistakes bowlers make include trying to hook the ball or changing their target when shooting spares with this system. The golden rule for alignment is to move your feet LEFT for RIGHT side spares and move your feet RIGHT for LEFT side spares, while always aiming at Arrow 4. Adjustments are made in increments of 3, 6, or 9 boards from your Zero Position. For example, to pick up the 2 pin, move 3 boards RIGHT; for the 4 pin, move 6 boards RIGHT; and for the 7 pin, move 9 boards RIGHT. Conversely, for the 3 pin, move LEFT 3 boards; for the 6 pin, move LEFT 6 boards, and move 9 boards left for the 10 pin.

Mini-drill: Find your Zero Position by throwing a straight ball at Arrow 4 to hit the head pin. Once found, practice moving 3, 6 and 9 boards in each direction to see how simple it is to spare single pins.
